name: Spicy Pork with Peanuts
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp Chicken broth, cold
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir-fry the peanuts over a high flame in 1 tablespoon of oil until they develop a golden-brown color.
- This brings out their flavor. Remove them and set aside.
2. Lower the flame to medium. Add the remaining oil and season with the peppers.
3. Add the scallions and garlic, stirring for 10-20 seconds until their aroma rises from the wok.
4. Add the sauce ingredients: chile paste, dark soy sauce, sugar, Chinese rice wine, dry sherry, vinegar, sesame oil, and cornstarch mixed with cold chicken broth.
5. When the sauce comes to a boil, add it to the wok. The sauce should be thick enough to coat the pork pieces.
- Since the pork is not seasoned, the sauce serves as the seasoning.
6. Add the pork pieces and peanuts back to the wok.
7. Quickly toss everything together to ensure even coating.
8. Stir and toss for 30-45 seconds, or until the pork pieces are cooked through.
9. Turn the pork out onto a serving plate.
